assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a foreign-currency-denominated receivable/payable between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant because IFRS 9 permits a recognised asset to be hedged for a particular risk, and specifically allows foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements when the exposure is not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant because IFRS 9 also defines cash flow hedge accounting for variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.
